articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launched The Small Brief, an initiative bringing together three advertising industry icons to create breakthrough ads for local businesses they love using AI. This showcases practical applications of AI in creative advertising for small business marketing.

articles.title: How researchers are using GitHub Innovation Graph data to reveal the "digital complexity" of nations
articles.summary: Researchers are using GitHub data to predict GDP, inequality, and emissions in ways that traditional economic data misses. This demonstrates how developer activity data can provide insights into national economic indicators that conventional metrics overlook.

articles.title: Why age assurance laws matter for developers
articles.summary: Youth safety requirements are expanding to operating systems and app stores, raising new compliance questions for open source developers. These regulatory changes could significantly impact how developers build and distribute software.

articles.title: DOGE used ChatGPT in a way that was both dumb and illegal, judge rules
articles.summary: A federal judge ruled that DOGE's cancellation of over $100 million in grants was unconstitutional. The department used ChatGPT to determine if grants were related to DEI initiatives, which the court found violated proper procedures.

articles.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
articles.summary: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff, with CEO Matthew Prince citing AI efficiency gains as eliminating the need for many support roles. This comes despite the company achieving record-high revenue.

articles.title: Intel's comeback story is even wilder than it seems
articles.summary: Intel's stock has risen 490% over the past year as Wall Street bets on the company's turnaround. However, this dramatic increase may be running well ahead of Intel's actual recovery progress.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
articles.summary: In the second week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk's motivations came under scrutiny. Musk alleges that OpenAI's leadership deceived him into donating $38 million by promising to maintain certain principles.

articles.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
articles.summary: Amazon Prime Video is joining Netflix and Disney Plus in adding a vertical "Clips" feed to its streaming app. The feature offers short-form videos from shows and movies with options to jump into the full title or purchase content.

articles.title: ABC and Disney accuse Trump admin of violating First Amendment rights
articles.summary: ABC is accusing the FCC of violating its First Amendment rights through major policy shifts that the network claims will chill free speech. The dispute involves the agency's approach to the bona fide news interview exemption for The View.

articles.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
articles.summary: Google's AI search will start citing its sources in several new ways through AI Overviews. This represents a course correction to address concerns about attribution and traffic to original content creators.

articles.title: The new Wild West of AI kids' toys
articles.summary: Connected AI companions for children could disrupt everything from make-believe to bedtime stories. The unregulated nature of these toys has prompted some lawmakers to consider banning them due to safety and privacy concerns.

articles.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
articles.summary: Oracle workers who were laid off attempted to negotiate better severance packages but were refused. Some discovered they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections because the company had classified them as remote workers.

articles.title: Sony says "efficient" AI tools will lead to even more games flooding the market
articles.summary: Sony predicts that efficient AI tools will result in even more games flooding the market. However, the PlayStation maker emphasizes that human artists "must remain at the center" of game development.

articles.title: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
articles.summary: Simon Willison explores how HTML remains surprisingly effective when working with Claude's code generation capabilities. The post examines the practical advantages of using HTML in AI-assisted development workflows.
